Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

I think we want to continue testing that conf_param works for some time, since existing scripts/users will be using that interface.

I think that splitting this patch into two parts would be useful:
- adding/modifiing a subset of tests using "conf_param" today
- submit (but don't land) the patch to remove the remaining conf_parm
  and then we can land it after a few releases with "set_param -P"

Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

Currently this patch is just to test lctl set_param -P and not aimed for landing. As you can see only one test failed and I will look to see if I did something wrong with the test or if its a real bug. In any case this code is shaping up well.

We could approach this like the GSS work. Have something like CONF_PARM="false" to  be able to switch between both versions for testing. By default it could be "true". I just need to hook it into the Test-Parameter approach.

commit.subject: LU-7004 tests: move from lctl conf_param to lctl set_param -P
commit.message:
LU-7004 tests: move from lctl conf_param to lctl set_param -P

To ensure that the work to make lctl set_param -P functional
we need to provide a way to test either lctl conf_param or
lctl set_param -P in the test suite.
